comments.description: Single‑line comments in MQL4 start with "//" and end with the newline. Multi‑line comments start with "/*" and end with "*/"; nested single‑line comments may appear inside multi‑line comments. Comments may occur anywhere that whitespace is permitted

identifiers.rules: Identifiers cannot start with a digit and must not coincide with a reserved word

arithmetic.description: MQL4 supports additive and multiplicative arithmetic operators (sum, difference, negation, product, quotient and remainder of division) and increment/decrement operations

assignment.description: Assignment operations return the value of the left operand after assignment. Compound assignments combine an arithmetic or bitwise operation with assignment

relational.description: Relational operators compare numeric values; the result of a comparison is 1 (TRUE) or 0 (FALSE)

logical.description: Logical NOT negates a boolean value; logical OR returns TRUE if either operand is true; logical AND returns TRUE only if both operands are true; MQL4 uses short‑circuit (brief estimate) evaluation

bitwise.description: Bitwise complement (~), right shift (>>), left shift (<<), AND (&), OR (|) and XOR (^) operate on the bit representations of integer values

ternary.description: Ternary conditional operator returns one of two expressions depending on a boolean condition

other.description: Other operations include the sizeof operator, object creation and deletion (new/delete) and dynamic casting

functions.return_operator: The return operator terminates a function and optionally returns a value; the returned expression is converted to the function return type
functions.overloading: Functions can be overloaded by defining functions with the same name but different parameter lists
functions.default_parameters: Parameters may have default values; once a parameter has a default value, all subsequent parameters must also have default values

variables.declaration: Variables must be declared with a type and unique name before use; the declaration is not an operator
variables.arrays: Arrays are indexed sequences of elements of the same type. MQL4 supports up to four‑dimensional arrays; indices start at 0

scope_and_storage.local: Declared within a function or block and valid only inside that scope
scope_and_storage.static: Static variables retain their values between function calls
scope_and_storage.global: Declared outside functions and accessible throughout the program
scope_and_storage.input: Input variables receive values from the EA/indicator property dialog; they are read‑only
scope_and_storage.const: Declares a constant that cannot be changed after initialization

preprocessor.description: Preprocessor directives start with '#'. They can define constants, set program properties, include files, import functions and conditionally compile sections of code

oop.abstract_classes: MQL4 supports the creation of abstract classes with pure virtual functions

comments.description: Comments in MQL5 use the same syntax as MQL4: // for single‑line and /* */ for multi‑line comments

identifiers.rules: Identifiers cannot start with a digit and must not be reserved words

special_types.description: complex number consisting of real and imaginary parts
matrix type used for mathematical operations on matrices
vector type used for mathematical operations on vectors

arithmetic.description: MQL5 supports the same arithmetic operators as MQL4

assignment.description: Compound assignments combine an arithmetic or bitwise operation with assignment

relational.description: Relational operators compare numeric values and return true (1) or false (0)

logical.description: Logical NOT, OR and AND operators with short‑circuit evaluation

bitwise.description: Bitwise complement, shifts, AND, OR and XOR on integer values

other.description: Additional operators include sizeof, object creation/deletion, dynamic cast, packing structures and computing offsets

control_structures.namespaces: MQL5 introduces the namespace keyword to group identifiers into named scopes

functions.return_operator: The return operator terminates a function and optionally returns a value converted to the return type
functions.default_parameters: Parameters may have default values; once a default is specified, all subsequent parameters must also have defaults

variables.declaration: Variables must be declared with a type and unique name before use
variables.arrays: Arrays are sequences of elements of the same type; MQL5 supports multi‑dimensional arrays and dynamic arrays via vector and matrix classes

scope_and_storage.local: Declared within a function or block and valid only there
scope_and_storage.static: Static variables retain values between calls
scope_and_storage.global: Declared outside functions and accessible throughout the program
scope_and_storage.input: Read‑only variables whose values are set via EA/indicator properties
scope_and_storage.group: group qualifier groups input variables into categories

preprocessor.description: Preprocessor directives control compilation: macro substitution, program properties, file inclusion, function import and conditional compilation

oop.abstract_classes: MQL5 supports abstract classes with pure virtual functions
oop.namespaces: The namespace keyword allows grouping declarations into named scopes to avoid name conflicts
